name: pylive

metadata.name: pylive
metadata.summary: Python remote control of Ableton Live

documentation:
# PyLive

PyLive is a framework for querying and controlling Ableton Live from a standalone Python script, mediated via Open Sound Control. Internally, it uses the same API as a Live Control Surface, which means it can do anything that a hardware control surface can do, including:

 - query and modify global parameters such as tempo, volume, pan, quantize, arrangement time
 - query and modify properties of tracks, clips, scenes and devices
 - trigger and stop clips and scenes

It can perform many of the operations described in the [AbletonOSC API](https://github.com/ideoforms/AbletonOSC). More comprehensive feature coverage is a work-in-progress.

If you are looking simply to send MIDI messages to Live, this module is not what you want. Instead, try setting up a [virtual MIDI bus](https://help.ableton.com/hc/en-us/articles/209774225-How-to-setup-a-virtual-MIDI-bus) and using [isobar](https://ideoforms.github.io/isobar/) to generate MIDI sequences.

**Note for legacy users:** As of 2023, pylive has been updated to to interface exclusively with [AbletonOSC](https://github.com/ideoforms/AbletonOSC) for Live 11 support. Legacy LiveOSC is no longer supported beyond [v0.2.2](https://github.com/ideoforms/pylive/releases/tag/v0.2.2).

## Requirements

* [Ableton Live 11+](http://www.ableton.com/live)
* [Python 3.7+](http://www.python.org)
* [AbletonOSC](https://github.com/ideoforms/AbletonOSC)

## Installation

From PyPi:

```
pip3 install pylive
```

Or to install the latest (pre-release) code from git:
```
git clone https://github.com/ideoforms/pylive.git
cd pylive
python3 setup.py install
```

To check that pylive is communicating successfully with Ableton Live, try running one of the [examples](examples), or run the test suite with:
```
python3 setup.py test
```

## Usage

```python
#------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Basic example of pylive usage: connect to the Live set, trigger a clip,
# and modulate some device parameters.
#------------------------------------------------------------------------
import live
import random

#------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Query the set's contents, and set its tempo to 110bpm.
#------------------------------------------------------------------------
set = live.Set(scan=True)
set.tempo = 110.0

#------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Each Set contains a list of Track objects.
#------------------------------------------------------------------------
track = set.tracks[0]
print("Track name '%s'" % track.name)

#------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Each Track contains a list of Clip objects.
#------------------------------------------------------------------------
clip = track.clips[0]
print("Clip name '%s', length %d beats" % (clip.name, clip.length))
clip.play()

#------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Mdulate the parameters of a Device object.
#------------------------------------------------------------------------
device = track.devices[0]
parameter = random.choice(device.parameters)
parameter.value = random.uniform(parameter.min, parameter.max)
print("Randomising parameter %s of device %s" % (parameter, device))
```

## Overview

To begin interacting with an Ableton Live set, the typical workflow is as follows. Live should normally be running on localhost, with LiveOSC enabled as a Control Surface.

* Create a `live.Set` object, passing `scan=True` to automatically index the tracks, clips and devices within the set
* Interact with Live by setting and getting properties on your `Set`:
  * `set.tempo`, `set.time`, `set.overdub` are global Set properties
  * `set.tracks` is a list of Track objects
  * `set.tracks[N].name`, `set.tracks[N].mute`, are Track properties
  * `set.tracks[N].clips` is a list of Clip objects (with empty slots containing `None`)
  * `set.tracks[N].devices` is a list of Device objects
  * `set.tracks[N].devices[M].parameters` is a list of Parameter objects

Getters and setters use Python's `@property` idiom, meaning that accessing `set.tempo` will query or update your Live set.

If you know that no other processes will interact with Live, set `set.caching = True` to cache properties such as tempo. This will query the Live set on the first instance, and subsequently return locally-stored values.

For further help, see `pydoc live`.

## Classes

* `Set`: Represents a single Ableton Live set in its entirety. 
* `Track`: A single Live track object. Contains `Device` and `Clip` objects. May be a member of a `Group`.
* `Group`: A grouped set of one or more `Track` objects.
* `Device`: An instrument or audio effect residing within a `Track`. Contains a number of `Parameter` objects.
* `Parameter`: An individual control parameter of a `Device`, with a fixed range and variable value.

## Limitations

Note that pylive is not intended for sending MIDI note events or control messages to a set. For MIDI controls, use a separate module such as [mido](https://mido.readthedocs.io).

functions.name: live.object.name_cache
documentation:
Decorator enabling pairs of set_XX/get_XX methods to cache their
values, to avoid repeatedly querying the Live set for values which we know
haven't changed.

If the 'caching' attribute is set of the object containing these 
methods, the values specifed in 'set' (or initially queried with
'get') will be stored again for the future.

This is used so that we can query (say) our Live tempo value once,
and assume that it will remain the same unless we apply a manual
set_tempo.

This will fall out of sync if the value of tempo is externally
changed!

classes.name: live.exceptions.LiveException
documentation: Base class for all Live-related errors

classes.name: live.exceptions.LiveConnectionError
documentation: Error establishing a connection to AbletonOSC.

classes.name: live.exceptions.LiveIOError
documentation: Error accessing a file descriptor.

classes.name: live.exceptions.LiveInvalidOperationException
documentation: Error performing an operation.

modules.name: live
documentation:
pylive
~~~~~~

A framework for controlling Ableton Live from a standalone Python script,
mediated via Open Sound Control.

USAGE
~~~~~

import live

set = live.Set()
set.scan(scan_clip_names = True)
set.tempo = 110.0

track = set.tracks[0]
print("track name %s" % track.name)

clip = track.clips[0]
print("clip name %s, length %d beats" % (clip.name, clip.length))
clip.play()

classes.name: live.classes.group.Group

methods.name: live.classes.group.Group.active_clips
documentation: Return a dictionary of all non-empty clipslots: { index : Clip, ... }
Represents a grouped set of Track objects.

functions.name: live.query.query
documentation: Helper methods to save instantiating an object when making calls.

classes.name: live.query.Query

methods.name: live.query.Query.stop
documentation: Terminate this query object and unbind from OSC listening.

methods.name: live.query.Query.cmd
documentation:
Send a Live command without expecting a response back:

live.cmd("/live/tempo", 110.0)

methods.name: live.query.Query.query

documentation:
Object responsible for passing OSC queries to the LiveOSC server,
parsing and proxying responses.

This object is a singleton, under the assumption that only one Live instance
can be running, so only one global Live Query object should be needed.

Following this assumption, static helper functions also exist:

    live.query(path, *args)
    live.cmd(path, *args)

classes.name: live.classes.scene.Scene

methods.name: live.classes.scene.Scene.__init__
documentation:
Create a new scene.

Arguments:
index -- index of this scene

methods.name: live.classes.scene.Scene.play
documentation: Start playing scene.

An object representing a single scene in a Live set.

Properties:
index -- index of this scene
name -- human-readable name

classes.name: live.classes.device.Device

methods.name: live.classes.device.Device.__init__
documentation:
Encapsulates a Live Device object.

Args:
    track (Track): The track that this device belongs to
    index (int): The index of this device in the track
    name (str): The name of the track in Live

methods.name: live.classes.device.Device.set
documentation: Helper function to return the set that this device resides within.

Represents an instrument or audio effect residing within a Track.
Contains one one or more Parameters.

Attributes:
    track: Track object that this Device resides within
    index: Numeric index of this device
    name: Human-readable name
    parameters: List of Parameter objects

classes.name: live.classes.clip.Clip

methods.name: live.classes.clip.Clip.__init__
documentation:
Create a new clip.

Args:
    track: A Track or Group object
    index: The index of this clip within the track
    name: The human-readable name of the clip
    length: Length of the clip, in beats

methods.name: live.classes.clip.Clip.play
documentation:
Start playing clip.
Must use clip_slot (not clip) as this is also used in group tracks, which have clip_slots without clips.

methods.name: live.classes.clip.Clip.stop
documentation: Stop playing clip.

methods.name: live.classes.clip.Clip.add_note
documentation:
Add a MIDI note event to this clip.

Args:
    pitch: The MIDI pitch of the note, where 60 = C3
    start_time: The floating-point start time in the clip, in beats
    duration: The floating-point duration of the note, in beats
    velocity: The MIDI velocity of the note, from 0..127
    mute: If True, mutes the note.
An object representing a single clip in a Live set.

classes.name: live.classes.parameter.Parameter

documentation:
Args:
    device: the Device object that this Parameter belongs to
    index: numerical index
    name: name, as specified by the device
    value: current value, within a parameter-specific range

methods.name: live.classes.parameter.Parameter.set
documentation: Returns the Set that this parameter resides within.

documentation:
# TODO: Not a great name / concept - revisit or remove
Return a list of all non-null clips.

methods.name: live.classes.track.Track.create_clip
documentation:
Create a new MIDI clip.

Args:
    clip_index: The index of the clip slot to create the clip in.
    length: The floating-point length of the clip, in beats.

Returns:
    Clip: The new clip

Raises:
    LiveInvalidOperationException: If the clip already exists

methods.name: live.classes.track.Track.delete_clip
documentation:
Delete the clip in a given slot.

Args:
    clip_index: The index of the clip slot.

Raises:
    LiveInvalidOperationException: If the clip already exists

methods.name: live.classes.track.Track.stop
documentation: Immediately stop the track from playing.

methods.name: live.classes.track.Track.get_device_named
documentation: Return the first device with a given name.
Query devices

methods.name: live.classes.track.Track.is_stopped
documentation:
Query whether the track is stopped.

Returns: True if stopped, False otherwise

methods.name: live.classes.track.Track.is_starting
documentation:
Query whether the track is in the middle of starting (e.g., flashing play icon).

Returns: True if starting, False otherwise

methods.name: live.classes.track.Track.is_playing
documentation:
Query whether the track is playing.

Returns: True if playing, False otherwise

methods.name: live.classes.track.Track.clip_playing
documentation: Return the currently playing Clip, or None.

methods.name: live.classes.track.Track.is_midi_track
documentation: Returns: True if the track is a MIDI track, False otherwise

methods.name: live.classes.track.Track.is_audio_track
documentation: Returns: True if the track is an audio track, False otherwise

Represents a single Track, either audio or MIDI.
Resides within a Set, and contains one or more Device and Clip objects.
May be contained within a Group.

classes.name: live.classes.set.Set

methods.name: live.classes.set.Set.__init__
documentation:
Create a new Set object.
If scan is True, automatically connects to Live and queries the full set of tracks,
clips and devices in the set.

Args:
    scan: If True, automatically scans the contents of the set.

methods.name: live.classes.set.Set.scan
documentation:
Queries the currently-open Live set and populates this Set's list of
tracks, clips, devices etc.

Args:
    mode: Can be one of "auto", "file" or "network".
          "file" exports the set's contents using a local .json file.
                 This is the fastest approach, but does not work if Live is running on a different computer.
          "network" queries the set's contents via OSC.
                 This is slower, but can be used if Live is on a different computer.
          "auto" uses "file" for a local install, and "network" for a remote instance.
SCAN

methods.name: live.classes.set.Set._scan_via_network
documentation:
Interrogates the currently open Ableton Live set for its structure:
number of tracks, clips, scenes, etc.

For speed, certain elements are not scanned by default.

Args:
    scan_device_parameters: Queries tracks for devices and their corresponding parameters

methods.name: live.classes.set.Set._scan_via_file
documentation:
Scans the contents of the Live set by exporting the song structure to a local .json file.
Note that this will not work if the Live set is running on another system, i.e. over a network.

methods.name: live.classes.set.Set.load_or_scan
documentation: Load from file; if file does not exist, scan, then save.

methods.name: live.classes.set.Set.load
documentation: Read a saved Set structure from disk.

methods.name: live.classes.set.Set.save
documentation:
Save the current Set structure to disk.
Use to avoid the lengthy scan() process.
TODO: Add a __reduce__ function to do this in an idiomatic way.
TODO: Do we still need this now scanning is fast?

methods.name: live.classes.set.Set.dump
documentation:
Dump the current Set structure to stdout, showing the hierarchy of
Group, Track, Clip, Device and Parameter objects.

methods.name: live.classes.set.Set.open
documentation:
Open an Ableton project, either by the path to the Project directory or
to an .als file. Will search in the current directory and the contents of
the LIVE_ROOT environmental variable.

Will only work with OS X right now as it presupposes an /Applications/*.app
format for the Live app.

wait_for_startup = True: block until the set is loaded (waits for a LiveOSC trigger)

methods.name: live.classes.set.Set._get_last_opened_set_filename
documentation:
Parse Live's Log.txt to obtain the pathname of the last-opened set.
Tested on Live 11.2.

Returns:
    str: The absolute path to the last-opened set, or None if no log entry
         is found. Note that the set may not still be open!

methods.name: live.classes.set.Set.get_open_set_filename
documentation:
Returns the absolute path to the currently-open Ableton Live set
based on inspecting Live's Log.txt, or None if Live not open.

Only presently supported on macOS, fixes welcomed.

Returns:
    str: The absolute pathname, or None if Live is not running.

methods.name: live.classes.set.Set.is_connected
documentation:
Test whether we can connect to Live.

Returns:
    bool: True if Live currently appears to be running, False otherwise.

methods.name: live.classes.set.Set.start_playing
documentation: Start/stop playback

methods.name: live.classes.set.Set.undo
documentation: Undo the last operation.

methods.name: live.classes.set.Set.redo
documentation: Redo the last undone operation.

methods.name: live.classes.set.Set.create_audio_track
documentation:
Creates a new audio track by index.

Args:
    track_index: The index of the track to create. If -1, creates after the last existing track.

methods.name: live.classes.set.Set.create_midi_track
documentation:
Creates a new MIDI track by index.

Args:
    track_index: The index of the track to create. If -1, creates after the last existing track.

methods.name: live.classes.set.Set.duplicate_track
documentation:
Duplicate a track.

Args:
    track_index: The index of the track to delete.

methods.name: live.classes.set.Set.delete_track
documentation:
Delete track by index.

Args:
    track_index: The index of the track to delete.

methods.name: live.classes.set.Set.delete_return_track
documentation:
Delete return track by index.

Args:
    track_index: The index of the return track to delete.

methods.name: live.classes.set.Set.get_track_named
documentation:
Returns the Track with the specified name, or None if not found.

Args:
    name: The name of the track to locate.

methods.name: live.classes.set.Set.get_group_named
documentation:
Returns the Group with the specified name, or None if not found.

Args:
    name: The name of the group to locate.

methods.name: live.classes.set.Set.create_scene
documentation:
Creates a new scene by an index.

Args:
    scene_index: The index of the scene to create. If -1, the scene is created after the last scene.

methods.name: live.classes.set.Set.delete_scene
documentation:
Delete the scene at the specified index.

Args:
    scene_index: The index of the scene to delete.

methods.name: live.classes.set.Set.prev_cue
documentation: Jump to the previous cue.
Cues
TODO: Refactor cues

methods.name: live.classes.set.Set.next_cue
documentation: Jump to the next cue.

Set represents an entire Live set. It communicates via OSC to Live,
which must be running AbletonOSC as an active control surface.

A Set contains a number of Track objects, which may optionally have a
parent Group. Each Track object contains one or more Clip objects, and
one or more Devices, each of which possess Parameters.

A Set object is initially unpopulated, and must interrogate the Live set
for its contents by calling the scan() method.
